고린도전서 10:1.content: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;

고린도전서 10:2.content: and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

고린도전서 10:3.content: and all ate the same spiritual food;

고린도전서 10:4.content: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

고린도전서 10:5.content: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.

고린도전서 10:6.content: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.

고린도전서 10:7.content: Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”

고린도전서 10:8.content: Let’s not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.

고린도전서 10:9.content: Let’s not test Christ, as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.

고린도전서 10:10.content: Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.

고린도전서 10:11.content: Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.

고린도전서 10:12.content: Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.

고린도전서 10:13.content: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

고린도전서 10:14.content: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

고린도전서 10:15.content: I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.

고린도전서 10:16.content: The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?

고린도전서 10:17.content: Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread.

고린도전서 10:18.content: Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?

고린도전서 10:19.content: What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?

고린도전서 10:20.content: But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.

고린도전서 10:21.content: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.

고린도전서 10:22.content: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

고린도전서 10:23.content: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up.

고린도전서 10:24.content: Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.

고린도전서 10:25.content: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,

고린도전서 10:26.content: for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.”

고린도전서 10:27.content: But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.

고린도전서 10:28.content: But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, with all its fullness.”

고린도전서 10:29.content: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?

고린도전서 10:30.content: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for something I give thanks for?

고린도전서 10:31.content: Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.

고린도전서 10:32.content: Give no occasion for stumbling, whether to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;

고린도전서 10:33.content: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.
